entity.summary: Seychelles-registered centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched in April 2018. Bilaxy suffered a serious ERC-20 hot-wallet compromise on August 28, 2021, suspended affected services, and subsequently began restoring withdrawals for affected assets. The official site and current market profiles remain available.
entity.notes: Launch is stored at month precision because major exchange profiles identify April 2018 without a consistently supported exact day. The 2021 hack is treated as a major incident rather than an exchange death because Bilaxy later restored withdrawals and remains publicly listed with a live official site.

events.title: Bilaxy launched
events.description: Bilaxy began operating as a centralized cryptocurrency exchange in April 2018.

events.title: Bilaxy hot wallet suffered a serious security breach
events.description: Bilaxy reported that its ERC-20 hot wallet had been compromised, suspended the website and withdrawals, and warned users not to deposit assets during the response.
events.notes: Reported loss estimates vary by source; HEI records the operational incident without fixing one disputed loss total in the entity record.

events.title: Bilaxy began restoring withdrawals after the hack
events.description: Bilaxy announced that withdrawals for affected tokens would be restored gradually, with token-specific recovery arrangements shown on withdrawal pages.
events.notes: The notice marks a phased service restoration rather than a claim that every affected asset was restored on the same day.

evidence.title: Bilaxy exchange profile
evidence.notes: Profile identifies Bilaxy as a centralized exchange launched in April 2018 and registered in Seychelles.

evidence.title: Bilaxy exchange suspends website after ERC-20 hot wallet hack
evidence.notes: Reports Bilaxy's announcement of the August 28 breach, website suspension, and warning against deposits.

evidence.title: Crypto exchange Bilaxy loses funds in hack
evidence.notes: Independent incident coverage corroborating the hot-wallet compromise and service disruption.

evidence.title: Gradually Restore Part of Tokens' Withdrawal Notice
evidence.notes: First-party notice documenting phased withdrawal restoration for tokens affected by the hacking incident.

evidence.title: Bilaxy official exchange site
evidence.notes: Current official domain used as one signal that the exchange remains publicly available.

evidence.title: Bilaxy current exchange statistics and markets
evidence.notes: Current exchange profile and market listing used alongside the official site to support active-side handling.
